_comment: Volunteer page copy. The form's technical field names/ids stay in code — this file holds the headings, committee cards, checkbox list, Q&A, and the thank-you message.

seo.title: Volunteer · Sakyadhita
seo.description: Sakyadhita runs on volunteers — 15 committees need help, from translation to web development to conference planning. Apply to volunteer from anywhere in the world.

hero.title: Sakyadhita runs on volunteers
hero.lede: We're a 501(c)3 non-profit, entirely volunteer-run. 15 committees need help — from translation to web development to conference planning.

committees.heading: Where you can help
committees.text: Each committee handles a specific part of Sakyadhita's work. Most accept volunteers worldwide and operate remotely.

items.title: Translation
items.text: Translate newsletters and communications into multiple languages.

items.title: Editing
items.text: Polish articles for the newsletter, conference proceedings, and website.

items.title: Writing
items.text: Draft blog posts, news updates, and member features.

items.title: Research
items.text: Document the lives and contributions of Buddhist women, historical and present.

items.title: Tech Support
items.text: Help with virtual meetings, digital platforms, and troubleshooting.

items.title: Programming
items.text: Develop web features, maintain the site, optimize infrastructure.

items.title: Design
items.text: Create graphics, layouts, and branding for print and digital.

items.title: Arts
items.text: Visual arts, photography, music, traditional crafts.

items.title: Conference Planning
items.text: Coordinate speakers, workshops, and logistics for the biennial conference.

items.title: Branches & Chapters
items.text: Help establish and support local Sakyadhita communities worldwide.

items.title: Ordination
items.text: Research, advocacy, and educational resources on the ordination movement.

formHead.heading: Volunteer with Sakyadhita
formHead.text: We're always in search of volunteers — and especially in the years we hold our international conference. Whatever time or skills you can offer, we'd be so grateful to have you with us.

items.question: Who can volunteer?
items.answer: Anyone, anywhere. You don't need to be a member or a Buddhist — just a willingness to support our work. We welcome laywomen and men, monastics, students, and professionals of all backgrounds.

items.question: How much time does it take?
items.answer: It's entirely flexible. Some volunteers give a couple of hours around a single project or conference; others contribute regularly over years. You set a commitment that fits your life.

items.question: Can I volunteer remotely?
items.answer: Yes. Most committees operate entirely online and accept volunteers worldwide. A few roles connected to the biennial conference may involve in-person work, but these are clearly marked.

items.question: Do I need special skills?
items.answer: It depends on the committee. Some roles use specific skills — translation, web development, accounting — while others need only enthusiasm and reliability. Tell us your strengths and we'll find a good fit.

items.question: Is volunteering paid?
items.answer: Sakyadhita is a non-profit run entirely by volunteers, so roles are unpaid. What you gain is a global community, meaningful work, and a direct hand in supporting Buddhist women worldwide.

items.question: How do I get started?
items.answer: Apply with the form above and let us know which committees interest you and roughly how much time you can offer. A coordinator will follow up to talk through where you'd like to help.

success.title: Application received
success.text: Thank you for offering your time! A volunteer coordinator will follow up to talk through where you can help.
